title: Capturing Screen State
concept: Taking viewport screenshots at regular intervals as the primary observation feed for desktop agents.

exercises.prompt: What can you use to control applications on your desktop?
exercises.explain: The correct answer is 'Mouse and keyboard' because they are the primary tools we use to control desktop applications.

cards.title: Why Capture Screen State?
cards.body: Capturing the screen lets you take snapshots of what’s happening on your device. This helps you analyze events or automate tasks better!

cards.title: Screen State
cards.body: The screen state is the visual information displayed on your screen. Capturing it means taking a picture of this information at a certain time.

cards.title: Like a Camera
cards.body: Capturing your screen is like taking a photo of a busy street. You can see what’s happening at that moment, just like a snapshot of your computer!

cards.title: Regular Interval Screenshots
cards.body: Think of taking a screenshot every second. This forms a series of images that allow you to see changes over time.

cards.title: Myth: You Only Need One Screenshot
cards.body: Some think capturing the screen once is enough, but that's like watching a movie only once. You miss all the action in between!

cards.title: Visual Comparisons
cards.body: Look at these two screenshots taken with time in between.

left.text: An empty desktop before any actions.

right.text: The desktop with new files and activities.

exercises.prompt: What is the main purpose of capturing the screen state?
exercises.explain: Capturing the screen state helps observe changes over time. It's like watching how things develop on your desktop!

exercises.prompt: True or False: Capturing the screen state is only necessary when making major changes to your desktop.
exercises.explain: False! Capturing the screen state is useful even for small tasks, as it helps track all changes.

exercises.prompt: Capturing the screen state allows us to take __________ of what’s happening on our device.
exercises.sentence: Capturing the screen state allows us to take snapshots of what’s happening on our device.
exercises.explain: The blank must be filled with 'snapshots' because it describes the images taken of the screen.

exercises.prompt: Which statement about capturing screen state is true?

options.text: It can help identify problems by showing what was on the screen.
It freezes your computer during the process.

exercises.explain: Capturing the screen state shows exactly what was happening on the screen, which can help identify issues or understand changes.

exercises.prompt: Why is capturing the screen state at regular intervals beneficial?

options.text: It provides a detailed view of changes over time.
It reduces the number of applications running.

exercises.explain: Capturing screenshots at regular intervals gives a detailed view of changes over time, allowing for better analysis of actions taken on the desktop.
